title: Sicilian Defense: Najdorf Variation
summary: Master the aggressive Najdorf, where Black creates a powerful kingside attack at the cost of pawn structure weaknesses.
description: The Najdorf is one of Black's most dangerous weapons against 1.e4. You'll learn why the move ...a5 (the English Attack) is so feared, and how to navigate the complex positions that arise. This tutorial covers multiple patterns: the English Attack, the Polugayevsky Variation, and typical endgame structures.

variations.englishAttack: 6.Bg5 e6 7.f4 - White pushes kingside pawns for attack
variations.polugayevsky: 6.Bg5 e6 7.f4 h5 - Black counters with kingside pawn storm
variations.classical: 6.Be3 - A solid alternative avoiding the English Attack

objectives: Understand the strategic trade-off: Black gets attack, White gets structure
Learn the dangerous English Attack (6.Bg5 e6 7.f4) and how to meet it
Master the typical pawn breaks: ...e5, ...b5, and ...d5
Recognize the Endgame that arises in many Najdorf lines
plans: White's plan: Build a solid center, castle kingside, and attack on the queenside with b4-b5
If Black plays ...e5, consider Nde2 or Ndb5 to control the d5 square
The move h4-h5-h6 is a standard Kingside Attack pattern
In the endgame, Black's isolated pawn on d5 can become a target
commonMistakes: Do not underestimate Black's attack. The Najdorf is one of the most aggressive openings
Avoid moving the queen too early. Wait for Black to commit before launching counterattacks
Do not castle kingside too early if Black has already pushed f5-f4
The b5 break is White's main queenside weapon - don't forget about it
completionTitle: Complex battle with attacking chances for both
completionSummary: The Najdorf is a sharp, double-edged opening. You've learned the main patterns and plans from both sides.

steps.title: Control the center
steps.instruction: Play e4 to claim central space and open lines for your pieces.
steps.coaching: The Sicilian Najdorf is Black's most ambitious response to 1.e4, creating imbalanced positions.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Black plays the Sicilian
steps.instruction: Black strikes at the center with c5, creating an asymmetrical position.
steps.coaching: The Sicilian creates a race: Black attacks, White builds structure.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nf3, developing toward the center and attacking e5.
steps.coaching: Natural development is best. The knight controls important central squares.

steps.title: Black supports the center
steps.instruction: The pawn on d6 supports ...e5 and prepares ...Nf6.
steps.coaching: This is standard in most Sicilian lines. Black is setting up for piece play.

steps.title: Open the center
steps.instruction: Play d4 to open the position and gain a spatial advantage.
steps.coaching: This is the main line. White accepts the isolated pawn structure for better position.
steps.hint: Push the d-pawn to challenge c5.

steps.title: Black exchanges
steps.instruction: Black captures on d4, giving up the d-pawn for rapid development.
steps.coaching: This is the main idea of the Sicilian: pawn for development.

steps.title: Recapture and develop
steps.instruction: Take back with the knight, developing and controlling the center.
steps.coaching: White has a lead in development and central control. Slight edge.
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the knight.

steps.title: Black develops the knight
steps.instruction: The knight develops to f6, attacking e4.
steps.coaching: Black completes development and puts pressure on e4.

steps.title: Develop the other knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, completing development and controlling important squares.
steps.coaching: White continues development. The knight controls d5 and e4.
steps.hint: Develop the queen's knight to c3.

steps.title: The Najdorf move
steps.instruction: The pawn on a6 prepares ...b5 and protects the knight on c6.
steps.coaching: This is the Najdorf's signature move, preparing queenside expansion and defending b5.

steps.title: The English Attack setup
steps.instruction: Place the bishop on g5, putting pressure on f6 and preparing f4.
steps.coaching: This is the start of the English Attack, White's most dangerous continuation. It prepares a kingside pawn storm.
steps.hint: Move the bishop to g5, pinning the knight.

steps.title: Black plays the Najdorf structure
steps.instruction: The pawn on e6 supports d5 and prepares ...Be7.
steps.coaching: Black adopts the typical Najdorf pawn structure: pawns on e6, d6, c5.

steps.title: Begin the kingside attack
steps.instruction: Play f4 to build a pawn center and prepare the attack.
steps.coaching: This is the English Attack: White pushes f4 to support e5 and attack.

steps.title: Black develops the bishop
steps.instruction: The bishop develops to e7, defending the king and clearing a8.
steps.coaching: Black completes kingside development and prepares castling.

steps.title: Recapture with the knight
steps.instruction: Move the knight to f3, completing development.
steps.coaching: White has a strong central position and attacking chances on both wings.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.instruction: Black castles kingside, getting the king to safety.
steps.coaching: Both sides have now castled. The battle intensifies.

steps.title: Develop the bishop
steps.instruction: Move the bishop to e2, completing development.
steps.coaching: White develops the bishop to a square where it supports the kingside attack.

steps.title: Black's queenside expansion
steps.instruction: Black pushes b5, challenging White's queenside.
steps.coaching: This is Black's main queenside break in the Najdorf. It creates counterplay.

steps.title: Challenge Black's expansion
steps.instruction: Play a4 to challenge b5 and gain space on the queenside.
steps.coaching: White meets the queenside play with a4, contesting Black's expansion.
steps.hint: Push the a-pawn to challenge b5.

steps.title: Black advances on the queenside
steps.instruction: Black pushes b4, gaining queenside space.
steps.coaching: Black continues queenside expansion. The position is complex.

steps.title: Exchange on b4
steps.instruction: Capture on b4 with the a-pawn.
steps.coaching: White exchanges, gaining the b-file and removing Black's queenside pawn.

steps.title: Black recaptures
steps.instruction: Black recaptures with the a-pawn.
steps.coaching: Black gets an isolated pawn on a5 but keeps queenside pressure.
